title: Stored XSS
description:
A comment system stores user-submitted comments in a database and renders them directly in HTML without escaping. Any user visiting the page executes any script stored by an attacker.

Fix the `render_comments()` function to escape each comment before inserting it into the HTML.
hint: Apply `html.escape()` to each comment's text before inserting it into the HTML template.
explanation: Stored (persistent) XSS is more dangerous than reflected XSS because the payload is saved server-side and runs for every user who views the page. Always escape untrusted content at render time, not just at input time.
canonical_fix:
import html def render_comments(comments): items = ''.join(f'
  • {html.escape(c)}
    • ' for c in comments) return f'
      {items}
    '

attack_flow.label: Stored as-is
attack_flow.value: Server saves the raw HTML string without escaping

attack_flow.label: Result
attack_flow.value: Script runs in every visitor's browser — persistent, wormable

why_blocked: `html.escape()` applied at render time converts `<script>` to `<script>` before it enters the HTML. Even if the raw payload is stored in the database, it is always rendered as safe visible text rather than executable HTML.
